about.analytics.description: Share anonymous usage data to help improve Cradle.
about.analytics.share.description: Shares manually defined navigation and feature outcomes. Cradle never includes prompts, responses, code, file paths, resource IDs, raw errors, or screen recordings.
about.analytics.share.label: Share anonymous product usage
about.analytics.title: Product analytics
about.external.agentActions.description: Approved agent and tool actions may read or write the local paths covered by that action.
about.external.agentActions.label: Approved agent actions
about.external.claudeDir.description: When signed in with a Claude official subscription, conversations in Cradle are persisted into your ~/.claude directory. Cradle passes through this directory, sharing sessions and memory with Claude CLI.
about.external.claudeDir.label: Claude session data
about.external.cli.description: Installing the packaged command creates or removes a symlink only when you choose that action.
about.external.cli.label: Desktop CLI command
about.external.description: Some features intentionally touch paths outside Application Support so Cradle can work with your projects and local tools.
about.external.githubCliAuth.description: GitHub features may ask the local gh CLI for an auth token when needed. Cradle uses it in memory only and never writes or stores it.
about.external.githubCliAuth.label: GitHub CLI authentication
about.external.githubCliAuth.path: gh auth token (not persisted by Cradle)
about.external.nativeSkillRoots.descript: When enabled in Features, Cradle places capability entry points in compatible agent skill folders so agents can discover and use the capabilities Cradle provides.
about.external.nativeSkillRoots.label: Agent capability folders
about.external.skills.description: Cradle-owned skills may live in workspace or agent-specific Cradle namespaces for runtime compatibility.
about.external.skills.label: Skills
about.external.title: Places outside Application Support
about.external.workspace.description: Editing files, creating folders, or starting an ad hoc workspace writes to the workspace you selected or created.
about.external.workspace.label: Workspace files
about.notice.description: Cradle keeps its database, logs, plugins, and runtime files in its app data directory. The exceptions below describe where it may touch local projects and tools.
about.notice.title: Cradle may write outside its app data directory
about.page.description: Version, local storage boundaries, and filesystem impact.
about.page.title: About Cradle
about.readOnly.description: External Claude and Codex session import scans read source files and imports copies into Cradle-owned records.
about.readOnly.label: External app scans
about.storage.applicationSupport.descrip: Primary Cradle-owned location for database, logs, plugins, runtime files, and diagnostics.
about.storage.applicationSupport.label: App data directory
about.storage.database.description: Local SQLite database used by Cradle-owned records.
about.storage.database.fallback: cradle.db inside the app data directory
about.storage.database.label: Database

appearance.theme.info: Light and dark themes fit different environments. System mode follows your operating system preference.
appearance.theme.label: Theme
await.guide.bypass.body: Some CI checks do not actually gate merge — flaky linters, weekly snapshots, or required-on-paper jobs you intentionally ignore. A bypass rule matches a check by name pattern and tells Cradle to ignore it during polling. Required checks (the req badge) can never be bypassed; only optional checks (opt) can.
await.guide.bypass.title: Bypass rules tell Cradle which checks can be skipped
await.guide.description: How Session Await pauses a chat on an external condition and how bypass rules fit in.
await.guide.intro.body: Session Await lets a chat session stop and wait for something outside the agent loop — most often a GitHub CI run on a pull request or commit, a PR review verdict (approved, changes requested, or reviewed), or a human-driven manual trigger. While the await is open, the agent stays idle and Cradle keeps polling the source. The session resumes as soon as the condition resolves. The Await panel in the right aside shows every pending await with its current status.
await.guide.intro.title: Session Await pauses a chat until an external signal arrives
await.guide.label: About Session Await
await.guide.pattern.example1.desc: Match every check whose name starts with "PR Checklist".
await.guide.pattern.example2.desc: Match all checks inside the "lint" namespace (lint:eslint, lint:prettier, …).
await.guide.pattern.example3.desc: Match a single check by its exact name.
await.guide.pattern.example4.desc: Match any single-character prefix followed by "ci" (ci/1, ci/2, …).
await.guide.patterns.note: Patterns are case-sensitive and matched against the exact check name GitHub reports.
await.guide.patterns.title: Useful patterns
await.guide.scope.description: Rules live in the workspace's Cradle database. Removing or disabling a workspace does not affect bypass rules in other workspaces, and a rule never leaks past its owner/repo match.
await.guide.scope.title: Per-workspace scope
await.guide.step.apply: Applied to optional checks only — required checks (the req badge) cannot be bypassed; bypass only affects optional checks (opt).
await.guide.step.match: Matched by name — the pattern is a glob against the check name GitHub reports (* matches any sequence, ? matches a single character).
await.guide.step.scope: Scoped per workspace — rules only apply to awaits created in the workspace that owns them.
await.guide.steps.title: How a rule is applied
await.page.description: Configure CI check bypass rules for session awaits. Bypassed checks are skipped during polling and do not block await completion.
await.page.title: Await
await.rules.description: Define patterns for CI checks that should be automatically bypassed. Supports glob patterns (* and ?).
await.rules.empty: No bypass rules defined. Add a rule to automatically skip non-blocking CI checks.
await.rules.label: Bypass rules
await.workspace.description: Select which workspace to manage bypass rules for.
await.workspace.label: Workspace

features.blockCodexAppServerLogInserts.d: Install a trigger in Cradle's Codex app-server logs database that ignores new log rows and keeps the WAL from growing during noisy TRACE or DEBUG writes.

integrations.slackGuide.step.slashComman: Create a Slack slash command named /cradle. With Socket Mode enabled, Slack delivers the command through the app connection instead of the old standalone bridge.

remoteHosts.guide.intro: Pair another computer by creating a host enrollment there, then pasting its pairing string here. Relay is the default; use a direct URL or SSH only when the server is already reachable.
remoteHosts.guide.relayNote: Already reachable by SSH or a direct URL? Use the Advanced path in the pair wizard instead of a pairing string.
remoteHosts.guide.step1.detail: On the computer you want to reach, open Cradle → Settings → Remote Hosts → This computer, then add an enrollment. It prints a one-time pairing string.
remoteHosts.guide.step1.title: Create a host enrollment on the other computer
remoteHosts.guide.step2.detail: Choose Pair another computer, pick Relay, and paste the pairing string. The computer appears here once pairing completes.

remoteHosts.guide.step3.detail: Cradle connects to the remote Cradle Server over the relay. Expand the row to browse its workspaces and files.
remoteHosts.guide.step3.title: Connect and browse remote workspaces

serverEndpoint.externalGuide.description: Route the hosted web UI to a Cradle backend reachable through Tailscale.
serverEndpoint.externalGuide.label: External device preview
serverEndpoint.externalGuide.security.de: Cradle does not have real account authentication here yet. Use this only for previewing on trusted devices inside Tailscale; do not expose the API to the public internet.
serverEndpoint.externalGuide.step.connec: Connect the external device to the same Tailscale network.
serverEndpoint.externalGuide.step.reload: Run Test connection if you want a quick check, then choose Save and reload.
serverEndpoint.externalGuide.step.setUrl: Go to Settings > Server Endpoint, then set Backend API URL to the target machine's reachable API URL, for example http://<tailscale-ip>:21423.
serverEndpoint.externalGuide.step.startS: Start Cradle on the machine that owns the backend API, and make sure the API is reachable on its Tailscale IP.
serverEndpoint.externalGuide.title: Use Cradle from another device
serverEndpoint.fallback.description: Resetting removes the renderer override and returns to the app-provided endpoint.
serverEndpoint.fallback.label: Fallback
serverEndpoint.page.description: Override the backend API URL for this browser or Electron renderer. This setting is stored locally and remains editable when the server is unreachable.
serverEndpoint.page.title: Server Endpoint
serverEndpoint.test.unreachable: Could not reach /health at this URL.
serverEndpoint.url.description: Use an absolute URL such as http://127.0.0.1:21423.
serverEndpoint.url.label: Backend API URL
shortcut.appshotHotkey.description: Capture the frontmost app window into the active chat composer.
shortcut.appshotHotkey.label: AppShot
shortcut.builtIn.bottomPanel.description: Show or hide the bottom panel.
shortcut.builtIn.bottomPanel.label: Bottom panel
shortcut.builtIn.browserTabClose.descrip: Close the active Browser panel tab.
shortcut.builtIn.browserTabClose.label: Close Browser tab
shortcut.builtIn.browserTabSwitch.descri: Switch the active Browser panel tab.
shortcut.builtIn.browserTabSwitch.label: Browser panel tabs
shortcut.builtIn.chatAlternateSend.descr: Send with the alternate continuation behavior.
shortcut.builtIn.chatAlternateSend.label: Alternate send
shortcut.builtIn.chatMode.description: Toggle the runtime interaction mode in the composer.
shortcut.builtIn.chatMode.label: Composer mode
shortcut.builtIn.chatSend.label: Send message
shortcut.builtIn.closeSurface.label: Close surface
shortcut.builtIn.commandPalette.label: Command palette
shortcut.builtIn.contextual.description: Shortcuts that apply inside a specific editor, panel, or list.
shortcut.builtIn.contextual.title: Contextual shortcuts
shortcut.builtIn.cycleSurface.descriptio: Move to the next or previous surface.
shortcut.builtIn.cycleSurface.label: Cycle surfaces
shortcut.builtIn.externalTerminal.descri: Open an external terminal in the active workspace folder.
shortcut.builtIn.externalTerminal.label: External terminal
shortcut.builtIn.global.description: Application shortcuts available from the main Cradle window.
shortcut.builtIn.global.title: Built-in shortcuts
shortcut.builtIn.jarvis.label: Jarvis
shortcut.builtIn.keyboardShortcuts.label: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcut.builtIn.layoutFocus.description: Expand or collapse available sidebars around the active surface.
shortcut.builtIn.layoutFocus.label: Layout focus
shortcut.builtIn.newChat.label: New chat
shortcut.builtIn.quickOpen.description: Search sessions, issues, files, and commands.
shortcut.builtIn.quickOpen.label: Quick open
shortcut.builtIn.rightAside.description: Show or hide the right aside for the active surface.
shortcut.builtIn.rightAside.label: Right aside
shortcut.builtIn.settings.label: Settings
shortcut.builtIn.settingsClose.descripti: Close Settings while it is active.
shortcut.builtIn.settingsClose.label: Close Settings
shortcut.builtIn.sidebar.description: Show or hide the workspace sidebar.
shortcut.builtIn.sidebar.label: Sidebar
shortcut.builtIn.switchSurface.label: Switch surface
shortcut.builtIn.workspaceCopyPath.descr: Copy the selected workspace file path.
shortcut.builtIn.workspaceCopyPath.label: Copy file path
shortcut.configurable.description: Desktop-owned shortcuts that can be changed here.
shortcut.configurable.title: Configurable shortcuts
shortcut.file.description: Override registered commands with JSON rules. Keys support mod, cmd, ctrl, shift, alt, and optional when expressions with !, &&, ||, and parentheses.
shortcut.file.title: Keybindings file
shortcut.overlay.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcut.page.title: Shortcuts
shortcut.webNotice.description: These settings apply when Cradle runs as the desktop app.
sidebar.title: Settings
support.alert.description: Cradle does not upload diagnostics automatically in this private preview. Exported bundles stay on this machine until you send them through your invite channel.
support.alert.title: Private preview support is local-first
support.dataDirectory.description: Open the Cradle-owned data directory that stores the local database, logs, plugins, and runtime files.
support.dataDirectory.label: Cradle data directory
support.diagnostics.description: Flush local observability data and download a diagnostics JSON with runtime metadata, recent server logs, events, and incidents.
support.diagnostics.label: Diagnostics bundle
support.diagnostics.note: Local private-preview diagnostics export. Review before sharing; no automatic upload was performed.
support.feedbackChannel.description: Copy a private-preview handoff block you can paste into your invite channel, email, or direct message with the reviewed diagnostics file.
support.feedbackChannel.label: Private handoff
support.feedbackTemplate.description: Copy a compact feedback template with version, runtime, server URL, and diagnostics instructions.
support.feedbackTemplate.label: Feedback template
support.onboarding.description: Show the onboarding flow again to reconfigure initial setup preferences.
support.onboarding.label: Onboarding
support.page.description: Export local diagnostics, prepare private-preview feedback, and inspect Cradle-owned data before uninstalling.
support.page.title: Support
support.privateHandoff.step1: 1. Export diagnostics from Settings > Support and review the JSON locally.
support.privateHandoff.step2: 2. Send this feedback text through the private preview channel you were invited through.
support.privateHandoff.step3: 3. Attach the reviewed diagnostics JSON only if you are comfortable sharing its contents.
support.privateHandoff.title: # Private Preview Handoff
support.template.diagnosticsNote: Attach the local diagnostics JSON exported from Settings > Support only after reviewing it.
support.template.title: # Cradle Preview Feedback
support.uninstall.description: Use the OS uninstall flow to remove the app. Cradle-owned user data is retained by default so accidental uninstall does not delete work.
support.uninstall.label: Uninstall
worktrees.cleanup.description: Cradle removes the oldest unbound worktrees first when cleanup runs. Set either limit to 0 to disable that limit.
worktrees.cleanup.maxTotalSizeGb.descrip: Maximum total size in GB across active Cradle-managed worktrees.
worktrees.cleanup.maxTotalSizeGb.label: Max total size (GB)
worktrees.cleanup.maxWorktrees.descripti: Maximum number of Cradle-managed active worktrees to retain across all workspaces.
worktrees.cleanup.maxWorktrees.label: Max worktrees
worktrees.cleanup.title: Cleanup
worktrees.confirm.description: This removes "{{name}}" and deletes its local branch. Unmerged changes in that worktree will be discarded.
worktrees.confirm.title: Cleanup worktree?
worktrees.empty.description: New isolated sessions will appear here after Cradle creates their git worktrees.
worktrees.empty.title: No Cradle-managed worktrees on this machine.
worktrees.list.title: Cradle-managed worktrees
worktrees.page.description: Manage Cradle-created git worktrees across every workspace and free disk space when old isolated checkouts are no longer needed.
worktrees.page.title: Worktrees
